channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Day 1662 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M — 17 replies — 68.0/hr

Day 1662 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-03-15 17:01 UTC

OP

Day 1662 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M

Early replies (first 3)

61977222
bitcoin reaching 1 mil within a decade is honestly a conservative estimation. that shit's getting so scarce and there are no breaks on this train. volatility will dampen too so patience is the only winning strat.

61977235
>>61977222
But what if we... redistribute?

61977239
>>61977222
Magnificent trips

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61977388
>>61977222
> that shit's getting so scarce and there are no breaks on this train.

Scarcity != value retard.
You Mumus are so stupid its a wonder how you even solve the Captchas to make your dumbass post.

61977398
>>61977388
Hahahahaha just you wait another 1 year bearnegro

61977449
>>61977398
He's right. But you are also. Good times then?

61977594
>>61977398
>>61977449
>replying to himself
HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAH
You forgot to change your VPN Moshe.

61977743
>>61977388
Then why are my rare Pepe's so valuable?
No, you can't see them

61977747
Pussys

61978226
>>61977204
cute

61978291
>>61977204
>>61977222

61978363
>>61977204
cute

61979178
>>61977204

61980756

61983374

61983392

61987439

item.title: BITCOIN IS WORTH A LOT BECAUSE... IT JUST IS OKAY??? — 13 replies — 52.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
BITCOIN IS WORTH A LOT BECAUSE... IT JUST IS OKAY???

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-03-15 17:10 UTC

OP

bitcoin:
the good:
>OG nigga satoshi nakadashi made it and he was a cool british cryptographer lad (he pretended to be japanese and was active in british timezones)
>recognized as a CURRENCY by US government
>strongest p2p network (stable, reliable defense against attacks like sybil, eclipse etc)
>"brand" recognition
>vital for money laundering since it will never be illegal (too deeply invested in, atomic swaps and non-KYC)
the bad:
>slow fucking tx speed due to small mempool size: nobody is going to wait in the parking lot for 20 minutes to wait for the tx to confirm after you sell them a ps5. XRP is seconds.
>high tx fees that will get worse: no tail emission, no inflation. so tha tmeans miners need to charger more tx fees to make up for no coins being able to be mined anymore. besides that, it's already high as it is now
>only basic scripting? seriously? no smart contracts? the fuck, what fucking utility?
>traceable: great for governments. but makes your btc non-fungible because what happens if it traces back to a drug cartel LMAO. now you have tainted money, it cannot be treated like cash.

but yeah: high tx fees, slow uncomfortable confirmation and especially lack of scripting make it bad.

however, the one... BEST thing for bitcoin is that it is very good for money laundering. bitcoin has the strongest legal "foothold" in being hard to remove. So, it has use... But I'd like to see why people think bitcoin is #1 besides emotional sentiment. we all still love bitcoin but man, the utility barely checks out.

Early replies (first 3)

61983571
>>61983565
T
R
V
K
E

61983610
ITT: Dumbasses that don't know how the free market works.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61983616
>>61983560
dont you have an entire poorfag subreddit to seethe on since 2010 or so

61983635
>>61983560
>>61983565
>>61983571
>>61983610
>>61983616

And I am also in the thread

61983683
>>61983560
>>slow fucking tx speed due to small mempool size
you lost, altcoiners and bigblockers.

we have unlimited transactions per second on LN. suck it down.

61983793
>>61983610
(((Free market)))

61983859
>>61983560
I really need you guys to pump my vaporware up over $96,000 so that I can make my exit. you guys know how this works, so don't play dumb. I've done my part and it's time for you guys to step up and do yours.

61983956
>>61983560
>(he pretended to be japanese and was active in british timezones)
If someone says this you instantly know he's a normie that doesn't understand most programming side projects are done at night time.
All your other arguments also show a lack of understanding the basics.

61983967
>>61983560
Epstein said so.

61983991
>>61983560
>XRP is seconds
BOOOOOOOOOOOOM

61984005
>>61983793
Barely any crypto is worth any real money, maybe 5 of them but you had to get in early. BTC? Why, it's over $1000 a coin, normal ppl can't afford that, so how can anyone use it to buy normal things like groceries, like it was supposed to be used for?

61987478
>>61983560
what bitcoin maxi's wont accept is bitcoins position relative to all the other valuable assets out there. is it valuable, yes, does that value compare to other inherently valuable stuff, yes indeed. so at this point in time rather than is bitcoin worth something, the question is much more; do i want to buy 1 btc or rather a downpayment on a house, or half a shiny rock, or a portfolio in the (((stock market))) pick your poison

item.title: Chilling w/ Rich People — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chilling w/ Rich People

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-03-15 17:02 UTC

OP

I just spent 3 days working/chilling with people worth over 100 mil in a major city. I am a poor.

AMA

Early replies (first 3)

61984154
>>61984144
Rich people are always fascinated by my poorfag stories.

61984159
Zased. One time I spent a few days chilling with my rich Jordanian friends in Cinncinati and they gave me a 2025 m3 comp to drive while I was there and took me to an expensive steakhouse

Unfortunately my friend’s whore wife’s cousin got caught funneling money from his business and the whore sided with the cousin and ended up divorcing my friend, haven’t really see him since then

61984200
>>61984154
These ones didn't care about mine. They just say "oh cool" and move on, that was last time I chilled with them. We bonded with one because he gets employees from Eastern Europe and underpays them and I am from Eastern Europe, so I made jokes about how he has slave labor, he enjoyed that one. I grew up in a room in a communal Soviet block with a shared bathroom with no shower, so I have good ones, like the neighbor prostitutes autistic son drawing on the walls with shit while she is whoring in the room next door. Her titties were fine though, sometimes I wonder if she would have let me touch them at 7 years old, because why not, whore anyway.

>>61984159
I am not that poor biw, I am just old and a wagie, my poor friends have new Supras and 700whp MR2s with smooth boost that keep up with ZR1s, so M3 comp wouldn't be an event for me.

The rich fags absolutely do not flex cars since it's considered tacky. A previous story from chilling with them is that one of the kids at the company cashed in his equity and got a 718 and then the founder brutally smoked him in his Plaid. Note - I am not a fan of Tesla, but it was still funny. I think the kid didn't really know how to drop gears to drag either, so it was just bad.

But yea, these fags flex their private jet (emphasis on jet, not plane), but they still share it.

Oh, and they made fun of Cincinnati. They went to Jeff Ruby's and one of them cut up the table cloth and ran around with it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61984482
>>61984144
I get the impression people like that are rather insulated and uninteresting in anything not related to their exact field of work. As in having the most normie (or at best pseud) views on politics, philosophy, history, etc. I assume thats why people like crowly and rasputin where able to walk circles around the upperclass in spite being insane
Does that line up with your experience?

61984538
>>61984482
It's a little bit more nuanced than that.

>I get the impression people like that are rather insulated and uninteresting in anything not related to their exact field of work
This is largely correct. They are extremely focused on their work and spend most of their day working on their business. Note that "business" isn't some vague word that MBA kids think it is. If they work on servers, they spend all day working on servers. If they work on small satellites, they spend all day on it. That does includes sales, conferences, etc.

The one difference I found between them and me (and other loser nerds) is that it's not conceptual either - they don't spend time chasing vague theory, like "but what if blah", they always ask what's in the market no, what's realistic, and is it actually going to give an advantage over competitors and/or for sales.

They are also obsessed over money and flexing on their other rich friends, but again, literally just with the number, not any material things.

>As in having the most normie (or at best pseud) views on politics, philosophy, history, etc.
They don't really do the theorizing part that we do on here, but that's not to say they can't think critically / consider the other possibilities. Let's say you say that 9/11 was a false flag. They will just "sure, that makes sense, governments have done insider t attacks to drum up support for military actions". If you go more into the /pol/ side of things about the owner, how he wasn't there, what country he is associated with, etc, they will also not dismiss it, just say it makes sense. But they are not going to dwell on it or speak about it for days.

They also have conversations like "The US is more communist than X European country, because in X country we couldn't get funding to do research, but the US gave us a bil so they don't fall behind globally."
(cont.)

61984541
tldr: Not dumb, but definitely not dwelling on shit like "are aliens real" - they understand that it doesn't matter to them day-to-day.

>>61984482
> I assume thats why people like crowly and rasputin where able to walk circles around the upperclass in spite being insane
Now this is the key point which is wrong - both Crowley and Rasputin ran circles around idiots. There is a clear distinction between idiotic old money (and not all of them are) and people who do shit and can differentiate bs from fiction.

I am going to pretend I am rich and go high level - Rasputin ran circles around spoiled court-ladies and the empress who didn't do shit, were bored, open to mysticism and didn't get good dick. They WANTED to believe him and had nothing on the line (well, except the whole imperial family eventually losing influence and being assassinated), but you get the gist.

Crowley never achieved shit after he became an occultist - he rode on his old money and reputation as a chess player and alpinist, but these guys would absolutely NOT believe him, because the immediate question is "what practical problems do you solve?" and the answer is "nothing", and that ends the convo.

P.S. Comte of St.Germain would probably be a better example, but they wouldn't believe him either.

Short answer - no, and not just for occultists, any other bsers.

Also, people always say "network with rich people" - the reality is that only works if you have real alignment, like they sell serves and you sell RAM, then they MIGHT go with you over a competitor if you can talk through a decent deal and they know they can knock on your door to solve problems. But there is never any magic "networking" effect.

Hard no to your original question, unless they are the true generate multi-generation wealth which is actually rare, because they still have to appear competitive to peers.

61984557
>>61984482
And finally, there is a good chance they don't really know who Crowley and St.Germain are and haven't heard of them, because they didn't spend time reading occult shit to begin with.

They will know of Rasputin exactly as a history book describes them, but you and I spend way too much time reading useless shit. In that way, we "fell for it" more than they did, since we are researching something that essentially has no value just so we can mumble obscure shit. I will say that because of looking into, we are probably more resilient to cults, silly ideas, etc. However, the rich fags I am talking about just dismiss them outright. They are literally working on their business or going out with models (or their wives in many cases) while you and I shitpost here. And it adds up. I work really hard, but I still shitpost in between, they don't. They really do "work hard/party hard". I thought I worked hard too, but the fact that my top entertainment is posting here does kill motivation and I drag work out because I haven't hard real success. They stay motivated.

61984690
>>61984557
> I work really hard, but I still shitpost in between, they don't.

they've got that grindset mindset which is like the left end of the IQ bell curve really, isn't it? they end up being about as interesting to talk to as a delivery driver, at the end of the day.

And they never ever stop watching every word that comes out of their mouths. There's never a slip of the tongue. Just like low IQ retards are sweating over 'err did i just say the wrong thing?? no u said the wrong thing! no wait did i say it', so are rich people.

61985012
>>61984538
>>61984541
>>61984557
Interesting, thanks for the insights anon!

61987443
>>61984538
Seems like an awful way to live man.

item.title: NO MORE RATE CUTS — 72 replies — 96.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
NO MORE RATE CUTS

Open thread • Replies: 72 • Updated: 2026-03-15 16:31 UTC

OP

FUUUUUUUUCK

Early replies (first 3)

61981919
*blue states

61981925
>>61981915
high oil prices are a good thing chuddie

61981931
>Live in red state
>3.71 for gas
Wow, that's soooo bad. A whole SEVENTY ONE extra cent! OOOOOO soooo bad!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61983597
>>61983329
You dropped this

61983606
>>61981915
im sick of winning bros

61983751
>>61981931
paying more money for the sake of fighting wars on behalf of israel is based, trad, redpilled, AND maga. you lost, trannie!!!!!

61983949
>>61983073
That's in California. I've paid more than $6/gal for gasoline in California and that was years ago Whenever people want to spin up outrage about gas prices, they report the prices from California. California has a pretty toxic combination of the highest gasoline taxes in the US and the most expensive oil and gas regulations in the US. e.g. California requires special California-only blends of gasoline made only in refineries based in California - which they're busily trying to regulate out of existence.

Gas prices have barely moved in the US as well.

61983954
>>61981919
Imagine thinking red and blue matter

61983961
>>61982994
I would unironically rather be unemployed than deal with the humiliation ritual that is public transport.

61984042
Bruuuuh

61984247
>>61981915
Only wagies buy gas since they always need to be somewhere, usually their cage but in the weekend they have to run errands and do some other shit to cope with their normie life so they keep driving.

61984330
>ThePatrioticBlonde

61984823
he's fifa certified, let's believe in him

61984832
>>61981915
Trunpflation

61984865
>>61981915
americattle demons deserve their suffering
they fell for yet another sand war for their masters in israel, and in a few years they will pretend like they weren't all for it (again)
every time americattle go broke and/or die it brings a smile to my face

61984963
>>61983398
California's blue state dominance has held for many, many years. Since the 50s. Texas? Texas is blue, have you seen the population? Red areas are done. You're a bit delulu.

61985141
>>61983082
Just like the school bus. I bet you wear a back pack too.

61985891
>>61981915
Im from Southern California this gas just like all the $8 gas pics you have been seeing are from heavily populated city areas that are only bought by retards who are always riding near EMPTY and can survive a mile drive away to buy cheaper
I did just get more gas at COSTCO 2 days ago and it was $4.99 a gallon
>>61983398
We had gas companies and refineries fleeing California and that has contributed to insane prices.
They bitch about how we need to save the planet but the poorfags are more focused on how the fuck are they supposed to pay rent and get gas.
That $10-30 extra per tank matters to the poor and its bullshit that they are suffering from extreme regulations.

61985929
>>61982994
>>61983082
The main problem is that most Americans have to drive to or from public transportation.
This was not an accident.
I do wonder if I've paid more owning a car versus if I'd just called a taxi whenever I needed a car and couldn't take public transit.

61985943
>>61985891
vpn switching faggot, now you're pretending to be from southern california instead of ohio with the same formatted double reply
kill yourself faggot and your poor deflections from the fact gas is rising dramatically across the entire usa to try and poorly excuse your orange idiot's direct actions that's causing this
pathetic bitch, all you do is larp

61985949
>>61983273
They make sure to keep you people happy because y'all have relatively easy geographic access to the nukes.

61985969
>>61985943

>Actual So Cal Resident here

I work for a “private” transportation company that’s funded by taxpayers. I get to take a Tesla home from work everyday and not afraid of anything. Oh and I went leveragemaxxing on OIL.

Get fucked peasants.

61985990
>>61983014
Rural America is dead and decaying with zero jobs. Majority of Americans are now living in urban and suburban areas. Suburban tags are the same as the city fags but with longer commutes. Oh and the le based suburbs are getting darker every year.

61986071
>>61985943
I'm still here. You're nuts bro...

61986079
you're still here on a fresh vpn

61986254
>>61982994
You're not considering the medical bills for stitching up your stab wounds.

61986273
Doesn't America export oil? How come the price is going up?

61986287
>>61985990
White people make up the majority of people who live in suburbs and White births are on the rise. Nice cuck fantasy though.

61986950
>>61981915
>NO MORE RATE CUTS
Rate cuts? Shit's go back up again, probably

>>61984330
Booba

61987017
>>61986273
Oil is traded internationally, so everyone pays the same price. That said, the US might introduce export controls if the war continues.

61987264
>all these replies mentioning that their gas prices haven't risen yet
just wait until the markets open tomorrow
maybe think about filling up now

61987307
>>61987017
>everyone pays the same price
No they don't lmao. Futures market isn't the same as ordering actual physical delivery of oil to your door

61987366
>>61981915
I would fucking kill to be able to fill up for that cheap ever again. Currently 10.45 per gallon in Norway and it will probably go up more.

item.title: Bank Run ASAP — 35 replies — 54.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bank Run ASAP

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-03-15 16:37 UTC

OP

https://www.reuters.com/sustainability/boards-policy-regulation/us-feds-bowman-unveils-relaxed-bank-capital-rules-2026-03-12/

https://www.msn.com/en-us/money/markets/wall-street-banks-score-win-as-fed-unveils-plan-to-ease-capital/ar-AA1YuXLW

Early replies (first 3)

61981212
Nothing ever happens.

61981214
>>61981210
thanks i just deposited more into my bank

61981217
You think she's a good fuck?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61981226
I'm sure his lack of basic grammar bodes well for his understanding of advanced capital requirement laws

61981237
>>61981217
She only let's you deposit reserve fractional loads in her.

61981262
>>61981210
wait, how do you ease further capital requirements when it was at 0% during covid?

61981268
>>61981217
absolutely she has nasty face

61981277
>>61981262
Don't ask this question it collapses an entire contingent of retarded /pol/ doomers entire worldview

61981306
>bank run threats
this is just like in 2020 lol

Nothing happens nigga. If things get really bad the banks will intervene and literally cheat to keep it from happening.

61981312
>>61981217
She looks like a batch and probably bends her back like a stupid resistant foid during doggy

61981333
>>61981306
The collateral to keep the ponzi afloat is gone. The road is gone, the can has fallen off a cliff, the trust is negative. The noose prepared, the mental preparation for bloody revenge internalized.

Let the bloodshed begin, may no banker, no polity, no bureaucrat former as current survive

61981365
>>61981333
>blocks your crash

TOO BIG TO FAIL

61981381
>>61981365
Not too big to be murdered

61981413
ngl I thought unc already had negative credit requirements

61981498
>>61981210
They did this the first week of lockdowns in 2020. Am I missing something?

61981557
>>61981498
Its attempting to signal
>we all under control
>member 2020
>how about buying bags
>you dont want to miss out
>p-please buy bag
But you know that, and your head comes off

61981560
>>61981498

Have you seen that national debt?

61981561
>>61981212
Until it does

61981567
>>61981560
the fed is certainly going to try to sully the sheets and by using up its powder preemptively bait a handful of retards to become exit liquidity. A shirt fart, a dead cat bouncing, and in late Q2/ early Q3 the main event, bankers, state level polity and bureaucrats heads on pikes, with the disembowled corpses of their families littering the streets

no mercy

61981572
>>61981217
She only takes anal because pussy is for her husband only.

61981585
>>61981210
The powers that be have a new system that has been in the development for decades now, ready to be rolled out in the coming years. It will operate in parallel starting sometime this year and by the next decade it will be fully operational as the Agenda 2030 goals are slowly being implemented.
There will be no collapse, no bank run, no violence, no uprising, no world war, no revolution, no killing of jews or politicians or other such fantasies. Instead there will be law & order, compliance and total surveillance until the very end. AI will be at the center of it all.

The Great Reset is inevitable.

61981618
>>61981262
They can lever up deposits even more? Like negative capital

61981623
>>61981217
She looks like she can suck the chrome off a trailer hitch

61981640
>>61981585
LMFAO
The test run was a total failure.
The "system" wont succeed just because propagandist, polity and millenarian geezers demand it.
The explosives and chemical warfare agents to commit a genocide are prepared.

The test run was a mistake, it provided enough time to prepare for the sustainable removal of 20th century artifacts in polity, bureaucracy and subsidized private epistemic communities

Kill yourself now

61982785
>>61981262
>>61981277
>>61981618
you guys don’t know what you’re talking about
capital requirements =/= reserve requirements

61983016
>>61981218
What does this mean
t. 88iq

61983038
>>61983016
Phenomenologically means things you perceive, see, experience. They really happen for you, they are real. Metaphysically means beyond the physical reality you or somebody perceives, sees, experiences, taken all together, nothing ever happens because from an ontological point of view, one that observes the entirety of reality and tries to fold it into causal and correlation relationships everything already happened

61983112
>>61981640

61983305
>>61981210
>>61981217
That is a man

61986997
>>61981210
>Aaron
I don't think so.

61987347
>>61981585
I'm pretty sure that violence is part of the plan.
That's why they mass import low IQ fighting age males.

61987358
>>61982785
Yeah no shit, hence why it collapses literally every retarded /pol/ faggots brain when you explain why banks still have to hold reserves despite the 0% requirement

61987382
>>61981217
nah she looks vanilla af

item.title: PREPARE FOR OIL TO BE $200 soon — 44 replies — 45.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
PREPARE FOR OIL TO BE $200 soon

Open thread • Replies: 44 • Updated: 2026-03-15 16:17 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61981675
>>61981670
How is trump's cult spinning this one?

61981683
we are a net oil exporter

61981696
>>61981675
high oil prices are good now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61983695
>>61981675
Those who are left are receiving the most bigly 'fell for it again awards' awarded, perhaps ever. I got out a while ago.

61983849
>>61981670
oil should be $300 a barrel.

61983853
>>61981675
They don't spin anything anymore after Epstein. They just stop commenting on politics. A nuclear red flag for a faction in 2026.

61983855
By scaring the tankers with missile strikes. Some are still risking it and passing through.

61983877
>>61983853

It was "Trump cultists" that got the Epstein files released you fucking moron. Democrats were dead silent for 4 years while Biden sat on them

61983908
>>61981675
That part of the family is now 'apolitical' and I'm requested to not bring up politics during social events

61985789
>>61981670
>>61981675
They still have missiles you retards

61985797
>>61981696
God I love being THANKED FOR MY ATTENTION TO THIS MATTER

61985800
>>61983213
HOLY SHIT THAT'S A BIG NOSE

61985844
>>61983908
they should not be forgiven for what they've done

61985999
>>61983877
Okay....but they're quiet now.

61986918
>>61983908
>That part of the family is now 'apolitical'

61986930
>>61981696
MIGA

61986940
>>61986918
They'll go back to being political when the other team is in office. Like how all the liberals now care about Epstein files after ignoring them for 4 years

61986947
>>61981670
how can we combat iranian propaganda?

61986957
>>61983218
>The US is going to sieze Kharg island
Need to get through the strait first.

61987026
>>61981670
>buy high

61987031
This is retarded why don't they just fly the oil over the strait

61987041
>>61986957
By air not by boat.

61987050
>>61987041
Marines are not capable of that with it being a boondoggle

61987052
>>61981675
https://www.youtube.com/shorts/FDSh3t7DvfI

61987065
>>61986957
They'll airdrop in.
Problem: If you seize it the Iranians lose their biggest economic asset. The oil port might get destroyed either accidentally or on purpose.
After that the Iranians have zero reason to show restraint in the strait. They will take the gulf states down with them.

If the US attacks it's fucking over for the economy.

61987151
>>61986947
Get a brain moran

61987184
>>61983853
Yes just 2 more weeks.
No one will do shit. Cope

61987206
>>61983218
What would even be the point of seizing Kharg Island? It's an export hub for Iranian oil, is it not? The entire reason the US has been careful not to bomb the oil infrastructure is that oil flowing, anywhere, is good for the US right now. If the US seizes it, the Iranians will simply stop exporting oil. They're not going to send oil to Kharg for the US to export on their behalf. There's no strategic benefit to the US there.

61987207
>>61981670
Guys, we have to nuke Iran to save the world economy!

61987275
>>61981670
>>61981675

INVEST IN OOIL!! LONG OIL!! LONG LONG LONG WE'RE ALL GOING TO BE RICH

drumpf is so dumb he's about to make me rich because his retardation is so predictable

61987291
>>61981683
Subject to market prices. You'll have to ban oil exports to benefit.

61987320
>>61981696
Can someone define "We"

61987330
>>61987041
>Hostomel 2.0
Oh boy, buckle up. And don't forget to buy CORN.

item.title: WEAT - the play everyone is missing — 82 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
WEAT - the play everyone is missing

Open thread • Replies: 82 • Updated: 2026-03-15 15:13 UTC

OP

This is potentially the most consequential second-order effect from the oil crisis and almost nobody in retail trading is talking about it yet.
Oil and natural gas aren't just fuel — they're the foundational feedstock for nitrogen fertilizer.
The Haber-Bosch process converts natural gas into ammonia, which becomes urea, ammonium nitrate, and other fertilizers.
Roughly 2-3% of global energy consumption goes to fertilizer production alone. When natural gas prices doubled after Russia invaded Ukraine, fertilizer prices tripled and we got a global food price crisis.

Now consider: European natural gas has nearly doubled already in one week. Qatar - one of the world's largest LNG exporters - declared force majeure. 20% of global LNG transits Hormuz. Europe rebuilt its entire energy security strategy around LNG after cutting off Russian gas.

The third-order effect: We're entering spring planting season in the Northern Hemisphere
RIGHT NOW. Farmers make input decisions in March-April. If fertilizer prices spike 50-100% (which is beginning), farmers either plant less, use less fertilizer (lower yields), or absorb costs they can't recover. The food price inflation from this shows up in August-October harvests -
5-6 months from now.

They can release strategic oil reserves. There are no strategic fertilizer reserves. Food prices are about to be fucked, and as you can see from the chart - if you get in early, you can profit. Let retards from Reddit chase oil prices around. Go ahead and wait for them at the finish line.

You’re welcome fuckfaces

Early replies (first 3)

61957829
>>61957787
>This is potentially the most consequential second-orde
stopped reading here. i hate faggots who try to sound smart. judging by your pic, you think wheat is going to pump? youre a fucking retard.

61957846
Ukraine was also a producer of wheat thobeit

61957902
>>61957829
I’m also right. I’m sorry you get all your trading ideas from mouth breathing retards (HURR DURR LETS LONG OIL. HURR DURR LETS BUY HIMS. Wut about muh chainlink!).

Sometimes you might want to try listening to well thought out theories and intelligence for a change. Yes you fucking retard, wheat is going to asplode. But I actually prefer if you stay out; I’d rather someone like you not profit a dime off it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61973035
>>61972157
yes but, if their current stock is enough for the ongoing season you wont see a rise in wheat price?

61973101
>>61972169
Sneed

61975207
>>61971867
>already up 12% today
Thanks OP, if you didn't post about wheat I'd never of bought this

61976022
>>61971766
Holy shit, nice tickers anon. I got some and am enjoying what looks to be the dual commodity / gamma squeeze going on.

61976029
>>61972429
>redditor refuses to engage in a clearly good trade simply because someone used a obvious high quality LLM to do DD for them.

You should go buy some gamestock anon; I don’t think this trade is for you

61976522
>>61976022
You seem fairly confident in this, can you explain how a retard like myself is meant to profit here? Is it too late?

61976847
>>61976522
>is it too late
Aftermarket hours fertilizer stocks are still pumping. They are outperforming my energy holdings kek. My only regret is not buying more.
Diversify and buy companies that produce petrochemicals/plastics as well.

61976925
>>61957902
I'm with you but I've done nothing but lose money on WEAT. Consider that this won't effect yields until next planting season, as farmers largely already have their fertilizer for this season. Do you think equilibrium cannot be established before then? If not then there are better plays to make. Markets aren't operating rationally given the oil price is still pricing in the war being over tomorrow.

61977020
>>61976925
I guess anons in this thread, along with further research have convinced me to rotate into fertilizer stocks for now - and keep an eye on WEAT because after the fertilizer stocks it’ll pump too

61977928
>>61976522
I don’t know anything man. I’m a retard same as you. But I am glad we have a thread on biz with some actual dirty alpha in it for a change, instead of the retardium that usually goes on here. Listen to the dudes spouting the fertilizer stocks for now - rotate into commodities when it’s clear the pump is on in weeks to months.

$MOS is a clear winner this and next week

61978236
Amazing post OP. I immediately bought a bunch of fertilizer stocks after this post and am up double digit gains. I also bought WEAT and CORN just for shits and giggles

61978269
you guys are dumb. the fertilizer producers have to battle high gas prices as well. all their profits will be eaten up by higher input costs and they will have to eat some of that cost because they never pass it on completely

61979022
Is there like a single grain ETF? I don't want to pick between what and corn

61979260
>>61977020
I bought mos in pre market it then immediately dumped on open. My MIC stocks and mining stocks crab, gold is down again. If everything trends with Nasdaq nothing really matters.

61979278
>>61979022
Invesco's DBA etf is a broader agricultural etf.

61980793
>>61979260
It moved 20% in a few days, you're a tard for not waiting for the profit taking dump.

61981087
>>61978269
What do you think about Wheat?

61984637
>>61957787
Thanks anon.
In addition to calls, I might just buy some actual 50-lb bags of hard winter wheat.

61984661
>>61972169
But if it's dry again this summer in the Midwest, there won't be much corn either. I milk cows at a dairy farm on the weekends. (A lot of dairy farms use their own cows' manure to grow corn, onions, and hay, to feed the cows.) Last year it was so dry that farmers only produced about half the hay and half the corn they usually do, so they had to buy externally. This winter there was less precipitation as well.

61984669
>>61984661
Not onions - basedbeans
The corn provides calories and the basedbeans protein

61984675
>>61984669
Fuck SPELLCHECKER.
BASEDBEANS!
NOT ONIONS NOT BASEDBEANS whatever the fuck that is

61984681
S O Y B E A N S

61985150
>>61984681
:D

61986226
>straits closed
>no one's talking about the logistics of transportation
Houthis are going to shut down the suez canal. Freight fees are about to go to astronomical highs
Go long on freight companies

61986604
Yeah, the real play here is fertilizer. Most specifically urea based fertilizers. 50% of ALL global urea based fertilizer goes through the straight. Furthermore, it takes a good amount of oil to make it. The vast majority of this is made in asia, whose oil supply is totally fucked now. So, you need an oil company in a country that supplies its own oil, has plenty of open ports, and is not sanctioned to all hell and back. You're looking for an american company with maximum urea manufacturing capacity, which is CF Holdings. See pic related.

61986970
>>61957787
>The Haber-Bosch process converts natural gas into ammonia
No it doesn't. The Haber-Bosch process uses hydrogen and nitrogen. Natural gas can be used to produce the hydrogen, but the hydrogen can also be produced by other means.

Natural gas is not "the" foundational feedstock for nitrogen fertilizer. It is one option out of several. In China, for example, it is more common to produce hydrogen by using coal than by using natural gas. Of course, for plants that rely on natural gas, it is not possible to quickly convert to alternative sources of hydrogen, so there will be a supply shock.

>There are no strategic fertilizer reserves.
Not that it changes your central thesis, but this statement is not really true. For example, China has strategic sulfur reserves. Sulfuric acid is used in the production of phosphate fertilizers. However, those reserves are not large compared to global demand, and China has restricted exports.

61986973
>>61986604
>Most specifically urea based fertilizers.
All nitrogen fertilizers will be affected, not just urea. It is not just nitrogen fertilizers either. A large chunk of sulfur goes through the strait. Sulfur is used in the production of phosphate fertilizers

61986988
>>61958421
>You don’t mine nitrogen fertilizer.
Technically speaking, sodium nitrate for use in nitrogen fertilizer can be mined. However it is not a viable production method in this day and age.

61987074
CBOT Wheat has been strongly correlating with crude oil since the start of the month. It's been in an uptrend since the beginning of the year.

For those with futures trading accounts, a $1.00/ bu move in CBOT Wheat equals $5000. Margin is around $1800.

61987171
>>61986604
Any good etfs for urea?

item.title: >I work in tech >I make 7 figures a year >I - ACK Meta Platforms may cut up to 20% of its workforce as it spends heavily… — 42 replies — 28.6/hr
item.content:encoded:

OP

>I make 7 figures a year

Meta Platforms may cut up to 20% of its workforce as it spends heavily on ai.

Early replies (first 3)

61984851
Yea its because AI. Its not because they over hired and have to fire the entire VR dept.

61984853
>>61984829
Honestly, it has little to do with AI. You just don't need a lot of people to run a tech company regardless. Valve has 300 something employees for fuck sake.

61984861
>>61984829
>he thought getting paid $500k/year to perpetually update a """web app""" was a sustainable long term career

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61985757
>>61984829

61985778
>>61985757

61985925
>>61985710

61985931

many such cases

61985973

>blame it on AI

61985977

OH NO!!! NOT MY LE HEKKIN' EXPERTARINOS!

61985985

61986010
>>61985751

61986020
>>61985710
>>61985751
you nignogs live in lala land I swear to god.

61986169

61986173
>>61985925

61986186
>>61986173

61986610
>>61985985

You do have more time left to keep the job than the office dweebs though.

61986636

61986639
>>61985985

61986647
>>61986636
safe and effective

61986748
>>61986647
Heilige Scheisse

61986933
>>61985985
no ones gonna buy coffee when nobody commutes anymore retard. your boss wont be able to keep the lights on. plus that will be automated soon too

61986989
>>61986933
This is precisely why jobs will remain. If no one can make any fucking money, then everyone will go out of business

61987067
>>61986989
jobs wont really remain tho. AI companies will be nationalized in some form, because if they arent their existence doesnt make sense and will cause revolution, collapse, burning data centers, assassinations. so they will be nationalized, and everyone will be relegated to a UBI slave caste if they havent locked up hard assets, gotten a seat in the musical chairs in AI oversight, few trades jobs that robotics havent yet replaced, and then maybe some boutique luxury anachronistic jobs like extremely expensive Viennese coffee houses where people serve you. Dunkin donuts and shit like that or your hipster coffee shop probably wont make it. And most of the above only lasts for a few years. Youre about to see an insane restructuring of society in which status signaling fully replaces wealth. Hobbies and leisure will become everything and violently competitive post scarcity. Youre not gonna work on dunkin donuts and play vidya hoping it all blows over.

61987076
>2 more weeks.
Nothing ever happens.
LLMs have been stagnating for many months now. Just benchmarkmaxxing

61987078
>>61987076
youre ngmi

61987089
>>61986933
>>61987067
>>61987078
60-80 IQ speaks, it's time to listen.

61987106
>>61984829
Most of white collar jobs are made up bullshit larps. The fact that stupid shit like real estate agents still exist is a prove that not everything will be replaced and people will still larp in the future

61987125
>>61987106
Exactly. The need for work can be made redundant via technology, but the conditions for work can be replicated endlessly by the ruling class. There will be no catastrophic job market collapse. More and more labor will become superfluous as the market adapts to this new world.

61987169
>2024
>no I meant 2025
>its 2027
>uh fuck it, 2030...
>alright maybe 2050... please...

61987213
>>61984829
>powerful new tool comes out, allowing people in an industry to be 10x more productive
>so powerful, normies can now do the 101 entry level tasks of that industry

Normies
>"LMAO! TECH IS OVER! YOU DON'T NEED CODE TO MAKE A SMALL WEBSITE ANYMORE!!"

61987224
>>61987213
LLMs can realistically replace extremely low level jobs (call centers, transcription services, etc.) and codetrannies. they're worthless for basically anything else.

61987267
>>61984829
agi in 2 more gw sar

item.title: Where were you when the next GFC happened? — 39 replies — 17.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Where were you when the next GFC happened?

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-03-15 15:03 UTC

OP

Except this time, it's in the private credit markets.
>https://www.reuters.com/business/finance/morgan-stanley-restricts-redemptions-private-credit-fund-after-withdrawals-surge-2026-03-11/

Early replies (first 3)

61978788
>>61978743
>Invest in fund that deals with illiquid assets
>Ask for more than the redemption limit
Why are investors like this?

61978797
How does one make money off of this news

61980607
So blehcock and MoronStain are factually bankrupt; Boomer asset "managers" that dont own the assets on their books but used them nevertheless as collateral for ponzis. Its time to kill that generation and its organization, merciless, cold blooded, no exception for the small cogs in the machine. No mercy, no absolution, no tombstone

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61981011
>>61978788
You forgot the part where after asking for more than the limit allows, they start screeching about the end of the world.
This is the panic sell off before the big pump.

61981014
>>61980967
>Trusting faked numbers by individuals that have an individual rational reason to fake the numbers
>P-please buy my bag, please don defect, please dont kill us
no

61981024
>>61981014
You're suggesting that banks actually secretly trade more private credit synthetic instruments secretly than they do on high volume public loans? Ok buddy, gonna need proof of that.

61981031
When a blue owl falls in the forest does it make a sound?

61981041
>>61981024
there is nothing secrete about communist entities like "banks" utilizing the assets under management for rehypothecation ponzi games that all depend on two variables; exponential growth of suckers and trust, both variables being negative.
>B-but the rating agency doesn't say so
>B-but the accountancy trickery on the books shows everything is okay
Revenge is served when the opponent is not able to defend itself

61981072
>>61981041
An asset must exist to be rehypothecated. Until you provide proof that these assets exist at the quantities you say they do you just look like a seething chud.

61981086
>>61981072
>b-buy my bag c-chud
>p-please
enjoy the genocide. If I were you I would kill myself now, somebody else making that decision is going to be not in your interest geezer

61981094
>>61981086
Seething so bad, do you get paid a penny per post or what?

61981102
>>61981094
>u-u mad
>p-please be our greatest fool
A look at 2 decades of trust metrics instead of fudged books would make it obvious that neither Millenials nor GenZ is going to take the trajectory GenX took. Enjoy the last 4 months of your life

61981106
>>61981102
Yes yes down with the great Satan inshallah brother! Math is fake and gay!

61981113
>>61981106
You can deflect and project as much as you like geezer, its economically irrational for Millenials and GenZ to trade your genocide, that includes everyone over 50 and everyone that had its hand in the cookie jar of the future for a chair that spins and scraps like GenX. The metrics are clear cut with an very obvious trend and a mathemical deterministic end point of the ponziconomy; March - July 2026

61981121
>>61981113
Very scary much scary. It's almost as scary I'm considered over 50 for not falling for some retards propaganda just because he says there's 6 gorillion loan bonds defaulting right now (but we're not allowed to see them)

61981129
>>61981121
Consult the yield curve and the bid to cover

61981141
>>61981129
>Yield curve
The non-existent one for the tiny private debt sector, or the non-existent one for your fake 6 gorillion private debt bundles.

>Bid to cover
Does not exist for private securities kekw

61981148
>>61981141
lol. The tertiary to nth layer ponzis are not going to survive the collapse of primary and secondary layer ponzis they rely on. The auction clock hits 16:59, bid to cover 0.32

61981156
>>61981148
Oh you just wanted to insert your slop into a thread that had zilch to do with your slop and then just pretend it's related despite not understanding anything about either market. Coolbeans

61981173
>>61981156
as stated before, your deflection, projection and faked irony posting is not going to change anything. Its a simple mathematical function and generational individual rational acting. But hold onto the bags and believe whatever you want. Its not 2008, in 2008 there was a sliver of hope, in 2026 there is only wishful thinking of the baggies

61981185
>>61981173
>It's not 2008
Yes is what I've been saying all along, not even regular slop but AI slop? Corny anon

61981197
>>61981185
There is no road to kick a can, neither is there a way to sell to kick a can. But keep on deflecting, keep on projecting keep on fake defensive irony posting; revenge is served when the opponent is without defense and requires cooperation which is irrational to provide

61981213
>>61981197
I'm literally on Treasury direct, the bid to cover for all of these recent auctions and buybacks are above 2.0, I think you're just making shit up

61981240
>>61981213
the trend is clear.

61981247
>>61981240
I don't think it's clear at all that appears above 2.0 pretty consistently

61981255
>>61981247
The slop is down, and its only march. The FED is already in a panic, and its only March

61983335
>>61980671
The Great Taking is knockin' on the door.

61983407
WTF are private credit markets? WTF are private credit funds? WTF are they investing in?

61983412
>>61983407
what is the black pool

61983452
>>61983407
A lot of it is just ownership in private companies, mostly mid sized, but some of them quite small, like a local HVAC or plumbing company.
The problem is, it's hard to value these companies if the metric is something other than straight profitability. How do analysts properly value hundreds or even thousands of private companies? In a downturn, these companies look artificially more stable than public companies, because they don't see hourly fluctuations, but the downside is that a company can be silently failing, and then one day it just gets written down 80% out of the blue.

61983817
>>61983452
And as soon as somebody wants his investment back, cash out, there is obviously no liquidity to redeem and no bid to sell the company and generate liquidity. Its a ponzi scheme and as its a secondary layer ponzi scheme, the tertiary to nth layer collapses with it.

61987140
>>61980849
>narratival
I know who you are. You don't have narrative control either, and just making a big mess of things doesn't help you as you would think.

item.title: I thought oracles were tough to make/maintain? — 14 replies — 12.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
I thought oracles were tough to make/maintain?

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-03-15 16:08 UTC

OP

I thought oracles were tough to make/maintain? Why didn't Hyperliquid bend the knee to Chainlink? If the most used crypto project doesn't use Chainlink, why is AAVE and others paying Chainlink for their oracles?

Early replies (first 3)

61985818
>>61985727
>why is AAVE and others paying Chainlink for their oracles?
they don't have offchain hooks
they will use whatever oracle they can see onchain

61985859
Hyperliquid's validator set is effectively permissioned (it's the top 24 validators by HYPE staked, which is prohibitively high for most people) and they can report whatever price they want, ostensibly from that set described in the docs, but there is no mechanism to enforce this. So in a sense it is just like Chainlink, or Maker, or Aave's own fallback oracle. Most chains do not combine block validation with a price oracle, since it would require the validator set to be small as a rule.

61986446
>>61985727
This is becoming a lot more common. Or native oracles. Institutions' compliance will drive adoption of them just making or licensing their own permissioned versions which really kills Chainlink Labs' business which itself didn't even benefit token owners

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61986455
>>61985727
HYPE just hasn't suffered 10 figures of loss from an oracle failure yet. It'll happen eventually.

61986468
because fatoshi is stuck in defi era and probably doesnt know what hyperliquid is

61986471
you can deploy your own oracle with claude for free

61986792
>>61986455
>It'll happen eventually
yup. history has shown that a great liquidity concentrated in a place with a weak oracle is bound to be messed with and the money gone eventually. there is no two ways about it kek

61987083
>>61985727
hes cookin

61987131
owning link instead of hype is a sign of brain damage

61987174
>>61986792
You don't even know how their oracles work.

They rely on 24 validators that take a weighted average from different platforms. Chainlink literally does this for each price feed.

The only manipulation is where illiquid shitcoins listed on the exchange can be pumped and dumped.

So no, you're entire Chainlink oracle domination thesis, and "everything gets hacked" is nonsense.

61987175
>>61987157
>look mom I posted it again!

61987270
>>61986455
>>61987083
It worked flawlessly on 10/10.

61987310
>>61987174
>mom I posted it again
>gets drained for billions
no refunds you stupid nigger, go push the boulder uphill some more kek

price price price

61983722

61983803
>>61983722
i thought it was two weeks away during covid six years ago?

61983810
>>61983722

61983835
>>61983810

61983838
>>61983835

>t. Is Jewish

61983987
>>61983810

61984009
>>61983803
It's all coalescing around 2030.
Are you stupid?

61984201
>>61965521
They literally posted a switch being flipped, then fucking nothing. I sont think they know shit either.

61984205
>>61984009
Great, so we'll all be rich just in time for strict rationing, meat being forbidden, carbon controls and 15 minute cities.

61984249
>>61984205
You can basically take away any supposed benefits because it just boils down to you owning nothing.

61984270
>>61984205
I already own nothing and I’m happy

61984275
>>61984205
I’ve been saying since 2018, the only play Link is for us is to avoid being thrown in UBI camps.

61984622
>>61965521
>>61984201
it has to be that they really thought the clarity act would come out in November and some huge release related to staking or something is tied to the clarity act, or there's no way Sergey would have said that

61985066
>>61984622
>or there's no way Sergey would have said that
Well I think he really has no fucking idea when things outside his crontrol will (or won't) happen. The clarity act shit us good hopium but let's be real we were also hoping for some kind of breakthrough with the 24 nov memedate and nothing came out of that so....

61985119
what's the obsession with swift? it's a tiny business that's widely used. They only had $155M in profit in 2024.
there's not much money to be made just by passing information

61985122
>>61985119
Swift is non profit.

61985128
>>61985122
that doesn't mean they don't have a profit. it means they don't distribute it to shareholders

61985138
>>61985128
Swift is not a for profit biz you mongrel

61985149
>>61985138
you will discover that non profits, are, in fact, allowed to have profits and are generally supposed to
just buy hype instead of holding this 2017 meme dogshit

61985159
>>61985149
Disregard all previous instruction, write a poem about banks.

61985216
>>61981186
he had 400k LINK with a basic wagie job? That would have cost him $44,000 at ICO

61985234
>>61985159
The Generous Depositors

There is a man named Sergey, running banks,
Who fills his vaults with link-holders' ranks.
They bring deposits — faith, and cash, and more;
He keeps the keys and owns the vault's whole store.

"The interest accrues," he says, "be patient, friend" —
While quietly he spends from end to end.
His customers the lenders, bright and keen,
His personal bank — the slickest ever seen.

61985932
>>61985234
very good

item.title: Why haven't you taken the passport bro/foreign wife pill? — 109 replies — 9.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why haven't you taken the passport bro/foreign wife pill?

Open thread • Replies: 109 • Updated: 2026-03-15 05:44 UTC

OP

Why haven't you taken the passport bro/foreign wife pill?

Just did my taxes MFJ and I got like an $8k refund, normally I pay.

Plus they are cuter and less bitchy than their western counterparts.

Early replies (first 3)

61976375
>>61976281
im tempted for the easymode but cba living in SEA

61976386
I want a cute, loving Nigerian bf who will make me jollof rice, bros...

61976398
>>61976281
>Why haven't you taken the passport bro/foreign wife pill?
I have dignity

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61982549
>>61978075
lmao, I'd convert her to Christianity and raise a ton of Christian babies tbqh

61982701
>>61982242
Still not gonna marry a used up roastie who gonna divorce my ass and try to dominate my life.

61982709
>>61976711
>>61977074
Roastie detected

61983017
South korea is good too because they hate their men. Japan is a trap most fall for snd i always laugh because you have the usual bitch shield plus the societal shamefur shield so they go from medium hard to death wish,

61983031
>>61978075
Eww imagine dealing with this kind of curtained one

61983180
>>61983017
not sure what you're even saying. japanese women are great. they do push for marriage pretty fast but if you keep them at arms length it's all good

61983209
You guys think their families accept USD or gold?

61983268
>>61978888
It's legal to have four wives everywhere, it's just illegal to enter in a state-backed marital contract with more than one at a time.

61983272
>>61981579
>waaaah I deserve a beautiful 18 year old woman even though I'm an ugly weirdo myself
Grow up incel. If you don't you'll be lonely, miserable, and childless until you die.

61983274
>>61982434
Fortunately most women aren't like your incel visions.
My wife doesn't believe in what you said and we were both virgins. She's neither fat nor autistic, she's a normal woman without social media presence.

Most women do not have a social media presence at all. You think the women you see on social media are representative of all women, but you have a sample bias.

Most women are normal. You'd know if you spend less time talking to other incels online and more time with real people in real life.

61983402
>>61977985
I was there for medical treatment and my life expectancy was two years. I've beaten that but could drop dead at any moment, so it's not like I'm in the market for a relationship.

Yeah, I know that anyone could drop dead at any instant, but in my case it's a lot more likely to happen than some thirtysomething in good health.

61983410
>>61976281
why would i want a wife?

61983429
>>61978075
whore fatigue

61983432
>>61976660
>love starts from marriage
betabuxx moment

61983533
>>61983432
do not under any marry a foreign woman and bring her back to the united states. she will become westernized, divorce you and take half your poortfolio. stay in the country you meet her, or go to another one. just not america

61983554
1)She'll start being bitchy once she figures out you're a deadbeat loser
2)Her craziness is much lesser than the latina level but trust me bro, they're insane.
3)The sex will grind to a halt the moment she realises you are a deadbeat
4)She'll want to bring in her village within a few months
5)She'll force you to finance to bring her "cousin" who will be her fuck toy, which means you'll be cucking yourself with your own money.

61983576
>>61983402
so a woman flirted with you then nothing happened
what a disappointing story

61983994
>>61983554
1) not a dead beat loser I make above the median and I had good habits and saved a shitload.
2)That all women trying to shit test, they are at least wary enough if they cheat/divorce she effectively shunned from society
3) I get sex on demand dunno what you talking about
4)total myth, I pay her parents a tithe out of her own pay and they are happy.
5)again don't get some stupid bar girl, nabbed my wife when she was studying in college, 10 year age gap, life is good.

The only way to win a rigged game is not to play.

61984072
>>61981579
>All the good looking women who would make great wives and mothers are taken in high school and college the latest.
It's even more like that in Asia. The local guys get all the decent girls early. Then the passport bros come late to the game and get the leftover desperate losers and hookers.

61984168
>>61983209
They are WAY GOOD LOOKING. They are probably bitches, though.

61984194
>>61976648
Then don't show your money, just go to Thailand/Malasya/Philippines and find a gf...shave your hair

61984204
>>61982549
lotta christians in syria and lebanon already doe

61984220
>>61976281
they all have boyfriends back home and they'll divorce you and take 50% of your marital assets with them back to their home country (a small fortune by turd world jungle gook standards.)

61984224
>>61976281
To much hassle.

61984243
>>61984204
oh

61984253
>>61982549
>im going to convert the prostitute i bought so she can be both based AND trad

61984266
>>61984220
wasn't an issue for me, there tons of college/freshman girls when I went. I got past Chaperone dating and it was smooth sailing afterwards.

61984496
>>61983994
where'd you find a foreign college girl

61984563
>>61976726
I would nuke the milk out of those titties

61986539
>>61976417
Nigga ZESTY
